channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] NVIDIA/SkillSpector — Security scanner for AI agent skills. Detect vulnerabilities, malicious patterns, and security risks.
item.description: NVIDIA releases SkillSpector, a security scanner that detects vulnerabilities and malicious patterns in AI agent skills.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/msitarzewski — A complete AI agency at your fingertips - From frontend wizards to Reddit community ninjas, from whimsy injectors to reality checkers. Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables.
item.description: msitarzewski releases an AI agency framework featuring specialized agents with distinct roles and personality traits for diverse tasks.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/chatwoot — Open-source live-chat, email support, omni-channel desk. An alternative to Intercom, Zendesk, Salesforce Service Cloud etc. 🔥💬
item.description: Chatwoot is an open-source live-chat and customer support platform offering email support and omni-channel desk capabilities.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] kenn-io/agentsview — Local-first session intelligence and analytics for coding agents, supporting Claude Code, Codex, and more than 20 other agents. Also: 100x faster replacement for ccusage!
item.description: kenn-io/agentsview offers local-first session intelligence for 20+ coding agents including Claude Code and Codex, with 100x faster performance than ccusage.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] alchaincyf/zhangxuefeng-skill — 张雪峰.skill — 张雪峰的认知操作系统。高考志愿/考研/职业规划的实战思维框架。由女娲.skill生成。
item.description: GitHub repository presents a cognitive framework for Chinese educational and career planning, covering college entrance, graduate studies, and job guidance.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] hexo-ai/sia — SIA is a Self Improving AI framework to autonomously improve the performance of any AI system (Model / Agent) on a benchmark task.
item.description: SIA framework autonomously optimizes AI models and agents by self-improving performance on benchmark tasks.

item.title: [Hacker News] Pokémon Go Scans Trained the Navigation Tech for Military Drones
item.description: Pokémon Go Scans Trained the Navigation Tech for Military Drones

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Restless bandits with imperfect binary feedback: PCL-indexability analysis and computation
item.description: Study develops PCL-based framework for computing Whittle indices in restless bandits with binary feedback, improving resource allocation in spectrum access applications.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] To Intervene or Not: Guiding Inference-time Alignment with Probabilistic Model Blending
item.description: BlendIn framework improves inference-time LLM alignment by blending model knowledge proportionally weighted by reliability instead of using binary guidance decisions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Dual-Stance Evaluation of Sycophancy: The Structure of Agreement and the Limits of Intervention
item.description: Activation steering that reduces sycophancy in LLMs cannot differentiate between sycophantic and factual agreement, suppressing both equally.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Few-Shot Resampling for Scalable Statistically-Sound Data Mining
item.description: FewRS reduces resampling requirements from thousands to few datasets for statistically-sound data mining with rigorous false discovery guarantees.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] ProHiFlo: Hierarchical Flow Matching with Functional Guidance for De Novo Protein Generation
item.description: ProHiFlo, a hierarchical flow matching framework, improves de novo protein generation through coarse-to-fine generation and functional guidance, requiring fewer sampling steps.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Physics-informed generative AI for semiconductor manufacturing: Enforcing hard physical constraints in generative models by construction
item.description: Physics-informed generative models must enforce hard physical constraints by construction in semiconductor manufacturing, not through post-hoc filtering.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Mechanical Field Networks: Structured Neural Dynamics for Multivariate Systems
item.description: MF-Net learns interpretable dynamics for multivariate systems by representing variables in shared field states updated through learned mechanical relations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Bernstein-Schur Kernels: Random Features by Sketched Modulation and Radial Randomization
item.description: Bernstein-Schur kernels enable efficient random-feature construction for nonstationary kernels via sketched modulation and radial randomization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Loss Landscape Diagnosis for Gradient-Based Gray-Scott System Inversion: Disentangling the Roles of PINN Components
item.description: Direct backpropagation through Gray-Scott reveals loss landscape pathologies and clarifies distinct roles of neural networks in PINNs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] PermDoRA -- Understanding Adapter Interference in Language Models: Limits of Parameter-Space Geometry
item.description: Geometry-aware adapter merging provides no advantage over standard merging; adapter interference appears driven by nonlinear representations rather than parameter-space geometry.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Seeing Before Colliding: Anticipatory Safe RL with Frozen Vision-Language Models
item.description: VLM-Safe-RL framework uses frozen vision-language models to anticipate collisions in high-speed reinforcement learning tasks, outperforming existing constraint-aware methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A prior-free blind detection of information leakage from model predictions
item.description: Researchers develop a framework for detecting data leakage in ML models using only predictions, identifying three leakage types with matched detectors.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LakeFM: Toward a Foundation Model for Aquatic Ecosystems Using Irregular Multivariate Multi-depth Time Series Data
item.description: LakeFM is a foundation model that forecasts lake dynamics from irregular multivariate time-series data, generalizing across heterogeneous lakes.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Quantifying Subliminal Behavioral Transfer Ratios in Language Model Distillation
item.description: Study quantifies subliminal behavioral transfer in language model distillation, showing Llama-2 exhibits sharp threshold effects while Qwen2.5 demonstrates continuous transfer of undesirable traits.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Federated continual learning: A comprehensive survey on lifelong and privacy-preserving learning over distributed and non-stationary data
item.description: Survey of Federated Continual Learning, combining privacy-preserving distributed training with adaptation to non-stationary data in federated systems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] RoVE: Rotary Value Embeddings Attention for Relative Position-dependent Value Pathways
item.description: RoVE adds position-sensitivity to value vectors in attention mechanisms via rotation, improving language model performance on long-context and few-shot tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Least-Action-Guided Diffusion for Physical Extrapolation
item.description: LAPG combines diffusion models with least-action guidance to improve physical consistency in generative models when extrapolating beyond training distributions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] FreeBridge: Variational Schr\"odinger Bridges for Cellular Transition Dynamics
item.description: FreeBridge models cellular transitions between control and treated populations using constrained Schrödinger Bridges that respect observed morphological geometry.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] FlowBank: Query-Adaptive Agentic Workflows Optimization through Precompute-and-Reuse
item.description: FlowBank creates a reusable portfolio of LLM-based agentic workflows, selecting the most efficient one per query at inference time.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Learning from almost nothing: How neural networks survive heavy input corruption
item.description: Neural networks remain robust to >90% input corruption by learning nearest-class-mean prototypes.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Energy-Conserved Neural Pipelines: Attenuating Error Propagation in Modular Neural Networks via Physical Conservation Constraints
item.description: Enforcing energy conservation at module boundaries in neural pipelines reduces error propagation and improves accuracy under noisy conditions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SwiftCTS: Fast Cross-Design Prediction and Pareto Optimization of Clock Tree Metrics via Few-Shot Calibration
item.description: Machine learning model SwiftCTS accelerates clock tree synthesis by predicting design metrics with few-shot calibration in under one millisecond.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] GLACIER: A Multimodal Student-Teacher Foundation Model for Molecular Property Prediction
item.description: GLACIER integrates molecular graphs, SMILES, and descriptors using student-teacher learning for computationally efficient molecular property prediction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Recursive Binding on a Budget: Subspace Carving in Order-p Tensor Memories
item.description: Subspace Carving memory architecture enables deep recursive binding by combining tensor fidelity with constant memory.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Risk Under Pressure: Compute-Aware Evaluation of Adversarial Robustness in Language Models
item.description: Compute-aware framework evaluates LLM adversarial robustness using FLOPs instead of fixed query budgets to measure attack effort.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Signed Compression Progress on a Sealed Audit is Goodhart-Resistant
item.description: Researchers prove signed compression-progress rewards on sealed audits prevent indefinite reward accumulation without true audit performance improvement.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Mirror Descent Beyond Euclidean Stability: An Exponential Separation in Initialization Sensitivity
item.description: Study reveals non-quadratic mirror descent is exponentially sensitive to initialization, unlike gradient descent, affecting LLM post-training robustness.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LSTM-Based Detection of Structural Breaks in Property Insurance Loss Reserving: A Climate-Informed Approach
item.description: LSTM networks detect climate-driven structural breaks in insurance loss reserving with theoretical performance guarantees.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] CRUMB: Efficient Prior Fitted Network Inference via Distributionally Matched Context Batching
item.description: CRUMB improves prior-fitted network inference speed by clustering queries and selecting distributionally-matched training subsets without model retraining.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Mahalanobis-Guided Latent OOD Detection for Hybrid ES-DRL Control in Time-Varying Systems
item.description: Method uses Mahalanobis distance in VAE latent space to detect out-of-distribution scenarios and switch between RL and extremum-seeking controllers in particle accelerators.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] PoQ-Judge: A Multi-Architecture Evaluation Framework for Cost-Aware Proof-of-Quality in Decentralized LLM Inference
item.description: PoQ-Judge framework evaluates LLM output quality without references in decentralized networks, matching reference-based evaluation performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Structural Attention Tax: How Retrieval Format Hijacks In-Context Learning Independent of Content
item.description: RAG retrieval format hijacks LLM attention independent of semantic content relevance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] NightFeats @ MMU-RAGent NeurIPS 2025: A Context-Optimized Multi-Agent RAG System for the Text-to-Text Track
item.description: NightFeats, a multi-agent RAG system, won Best Dynamic Evaluation at NeurIPS 2025 by using transparent architecture, outperforming Claude-Sonnet and Nova-Pro.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Detecting AI-Generated Content on Social Media with Multi-modal Language Models
item.description: Researchers deployed a compact vision-language model for detecting AI-generated content on social media, achieving state-of-the-art performance across multiple platforms.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] One Jailbreak, Many Tongues: Learning Language-Insensitive Intention Representations for Multilingual Jailbreak Detection
item.description: MLJailDe detects jailbreak attacks on multilingual LLMs using back-translation augmentation and cross-lingual representation learning with 98.5% F1 score.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LatticeBridge: Rare-Event Sequential Inference for Faithful Structured Sequence Synthesis
item.description: LatticeBridge uses sequential Monte Carlo with instance-compiled constraints to improve faithful structured sequence generation beyond standard decoding baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Benchmarking Large Language Models for Safety Data Extraction
item.description: LLMs benchmarked for Safety Data Sheet extraction show Gemini 1.5 Pro leading at 84% accuracy, but all fall short of 90% threshold needed for industrial deployment.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Compatibility-Aware Dynamic Fine-Tuning for Large Language Models
item.description: CADFT improves language model fine-tuning by using compatibility signals to suppress high-variance gradients from incompatible training samples.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] BioDivergence: A Benchmark and Evaluation Framework for Hidden Contextual Contradictions in Biomedical Abstracts
item.description: BioDivergence introduces a framework with six-class taxonomy to distinguish context-dependent variations from true contradictions in biomedical research.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ProcessThinker: Enhancing Multi-modal Large Language Models Reasoning via Rollout-based Process Reward
item.description: ProcessThinker improves multimodal LLM reasoning by providing step-level process rewards through rollout sampling, without training explicit PRMs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] T2MM: An LLM Supported Architecture For Inquiry-Based Modeling
item.description: T2MM is an LLM architecture generating interactive models for science inquiry-based learning, outperforming baseline code-generation approaches.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Calibration Drift Under Reasoning: How Chain-of-Thought Budgets Induce Overconfidence in Large Language Models
item.description: Chain-of-Thought reasoning in LLMs can cause systematic overconfidence beyond optimal reasoning budgets, a phenomenon termed Calibration Drift Under Reasoning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] EverydayGPT: Confidence-Gated Routing for Efficient and Safe Hybrid GPT-RAG Conversational QA
item.description: EverydayGPT's confidence-gated routing skips expensive generation for 85% of queries, achieving 6.3x mean latency reduction with modest accuracy improvements.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Beyond Compaction: Structured Context Eviction for Long-Horizon Agents
item.description: Structured context eviction scheme enables long-horizon LLM agents to operate effectively with unbounded horizons by semantically managing token budget constraints.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Afrispeech Semantics: Evaluating Audio Semantic Reasoning in Spoken Language Models Across Domains and Accents
item.description: Study benchmarks audio language models on semantic reasoning tasks across accents and domains, revealing limitations in current evaluation methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LifeSentence: Language models can encode human life course trajectories from longitudinal panel data
item.description: LifeSentence, a 24B language model, predicts life outcomes from longitudinal data and recovers education, wage, and motherhood penalties.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Geometric Profile of Semantic Information in Text: Frame-Conditional Uniqueness and a Trade-Off Triangle for Scalar Summaries
item.description: Geometric framework measures semantic text content using sentence embeddings, proposing three-coordinate profile and proving inherent scalar measurement trade-offs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Every Act Has Its Price: Compressed Moral Composition in Frontier LLMs
item.description: Moral Trolley Arena benchmark reveals frontier LLMs combine moral evidence non-additively with compressed composition rather than simple summation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Energy-Efficient On-Device RAG on a Mobile NPU: System Design and Benchmark on Snapdragon X Elite
item.description: Snapdragon X Elite NPU accelerates on-device RAG pipelines with 4x faster query processing and 4x lower energy than CPU.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Sch\"utzen: Evaluating LLM Safety in Bulgarian and German Contexts
item.description: Schützen dataset evaluates LLM safety in German and Bulgarian, revealing cross-language differences in model safety behavior.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When More Documents Hurt RAG: Mitigating Vector Search Dilution with Domain-Scoped, Model-Agnostic Retrieval
item.description: MASDR-RAG addresses vector search dilution in RAG systems by using domain-scoped retrieval with organizational metadata, improving precision in large document collections.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Dynamics of Human and AI-Generated Language: How Semantics Fluctuates across Different Timescales
item.description: Semantic-timescale analysis reveals temporal patterns in how generic versus specific vocabulary is organized in human versus AI-generated speech.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Probing Accuracy Saturates, Fragility Resolves: A Complementary Metric for LLM Pre-Training Analysis
item.description: Fragility metric tracks how representation robustness evolves throughout pre-training, revealing structure where probe accuracy becomes insensitive.
